runtime_target: packet registry, routing table, and promotion decision board
operational_end_state: A reviewer can see source identity, authority, route, output impact, and next build decision.
core_modules: item registry | authority classifier | route selector | promotion decision
source_strategy: Use sources to move the project from description to warrant: method source, domain source, evaluation source, limitation source, and output-bridge source.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | promotion test: item cannot advance without identity, authority, route, and next action
current_build_status: limited public proof; needs stronger public-safe abstraction or new demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Create a public-safe abstraction that proves utility without exposing private material.
first_full_build: Add promotion-router examples showing how a packet becomes a room, paper, software instrument, source reservoir, archive item, or blocked record.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
